NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. LORD, who may abide in Your tabernacle? Who may dwell in Your holy hill?
NKJV.2: He who walks uprightly, And works righteousness, And speaks the truth in his heart;
NKJV.3: He who does not backbite with his tongue, Nor does evil to his neighbor, Nor does he take up a reproach against his friend;
NKJV.4: In whose eyes a vile person is despised, But he honors those who fear the LORD; He who swears to his own hurt and does not change;
NKJV.5: He who does not put out his money at usury, Nor does he take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things shall never be moved.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. LORD<S>3068</S>, who shall abide<S>1481</S> in thy tabernacle<S>168</S>? who shall dwell<S>7931</S> in thy holy<S>6944</S> hill<S>2022</S>? <sup>abide: Heb. sojourn</sup>
KJV.2: He that walketh<S>1980</S> uprightly<S>8549</S>, and worketh<S>6466</S> righteousness<S>6664</S>, and speaketh<S>1696</S> the truth<S>571</S> in his heart<S>3824</S>.
KJV.3: He that backbiteth<S>7270</S> not with his tongue<S>3956</S>, nor doeth<S>6213</S> evil<S>7451</S> to his neighbour<S>7453</S>, nor taketh up<S>5375</S> a reproach<S>2781</S> against his neighbour<S>7138</S>. <sup>taketh: or, receiveth, or, endureth</sup>
KJV.4: In whose eyes<S>5869</S> a vile person<S>3988</S> is contemned<S>959</S>; but he honoureth<S>3513</S> them that fear<S>3373</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. He that sweareth<S>7650</S> to his own hurt<S>7489</S>, and changeth<S>4171</S> not.
KJV.5: He that putteth not out<S>5414</S> his money<S>3701</S> to usury<S>5392</S>, nor taketh<S>3947</S> reward<S>7810</S> against the innocent<S>5355</S>. He that doeth<S>6213</S> these things shall never<S>5769</S> be moved<S>4131</S>.

ESV.1: O LORD, who shall sojourn in your tent? Who shall dwell on your holy hill?
ESV.2: He who walks blamelessly and does what is right and speaks truth in his heart;
ESV.3: who does not slander with his tongue and does no evil to his neighbor, nor takes up a reproach against his friend;
ESV.4: in whose eyes a vile person is despised, but who honors those who fear the LORD; who swears to his own hurt and does not change;
ESV.5: who does not put out his money at interest and does not take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things shall never be moved.

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. O LORD, who may abide in Your tent? Who may dwell on Your holy hill?
NASB.2: He who walks with integrity, and works righteousness, And speaks truth in his heart.
NASB.3: He does not slander with his tongue, Nor does evil to his neighbor, Nor takes up a reproach against his friend;
NASB.4: In whose eyes a reprobate is despised, But who honors those who fear the LORD; He swears to his own hurt and does not change;
NASB.5: He does not put out his money at interest, Nor does he take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things will never be shaken.

NLT.1:
Who may worship in your sanctuary, LORD?
Who may enter your presence on your holy hill?
NLT.2:
Those who lead blameless lives and do what is right,
speaking the truth from sincere hearts.
NLT.3:
Those who refuse to gossip
or harm their neighbors
or speak evil of their friends.
NLT.4:
Those who despise flagrant sinners,
and honor the faithful followers of the LORD,
and keep their promises even when it hurts.
NLT.5:
Those who lend money without charging interest,
and who cannot be bribed to lie about the innocent.
Such people will stand firm forever.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. Yahweh, who shall dwell in your sanctuary? Who shall live on your holy hill?
WEB.2: He who walks blamelessly does what is right, and speaks truth in his heart;
WEB.3: He who doesn’t slander with his tongue, nor does evil to his friend, nor casts slurs against his fellow man;
WEB.4: In whose eyes a vile man is despised, but who honors those who fear Yahweh; he who keeps an oath even when it hurts, and doesn’t change;
WEB.5: he who doesn’t lend out his money for usury, nor take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things shall never be shaken.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of David. [1] LORD, who can dwell in your tent? ⓗ
Who can live on your holy mountain? ⓘ
CSB17.2:
The one who lives blamelessly, practices righteousness,
and acknowledges the truth in his heart ⓙ  —
CSB17.3:
who does not slander with his tongue, ⓚ
who does not harm his friend
or discredit his neighbor, ⓛ
CSB17.4:
who despises the one rejected by the LORD [1]
but honors those who fear the LORD, ⓜ
who keeps his word whatever the cost, ⓝ
CSB17.5:
who does not lend his silver at interest ⓐ
or take a bribe against the innocent ⓑ  —
the one who does these things will never be shaken. ⓒ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. O LORD, who may lodge [as a guest] in Your tent?
Who may dwell [continually] on Your holy hill?
AMP.2:
He who walks with integrity and strength of character, and works righteousness,
And speaks and holds truth in his heart.
AMP.3:
He does not slander with his tongue,
Nor does evil to his neighbor,
Nor takes up a reproach against his friend;
AMP.4:
In his eyes an evil person is despised,
But he honors those who fear the LORD [and obediently worship Him with awe-inspired reverence and submissive wonder].
He keeps his word even to his own disadvantage and does not change it [for his own benefit];
AMP.5:
He does not put out his money at interest [to a fellow Israelite],
And does not take a bribe against the innocent.
He who does these things will never be shaken.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, who gets invited
to dinner at your place?
How do we get on your guest list?
MSG.3:
"Don't hurt your friend,
don't blame your neighbor;
MSG.4:
despise the despicable.
"Keep your word even when it costs you,
MSG.5:
make an honest living,
never take a bribe.
"You'll never get
blacklisted
if you live like this."

NIV.1:
Psalm 15
A psalm of David.
Lord, who may dwell in your sanctuary?
Who may live on your holy hill?
NIV.2:
He whose walk is blameless
and who does what is righteous,
who speaks the truth from his heart
NIV.3:
and has no slander on his tongue,
who does his neighbor no wrong
and casts no slur on his fellowman,
NIV.4:
who despises a vile man
but honors those who fear the Lord,
who keeps his oath
even when it hurts,
NIV.5:
who lends his money without usury
and does not accept a bribe against the innocent.
He who does these things
will never be shaken.

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, who may abide in your tent?
Who may dwell on your holy hill?
NRSVCE.2:
Those who walk blamelessly, and do what is right,
and speak the truth from their heart;
NRSVCE.3:
who do not slander with their tongue,
and do no evil to their friends,
nor take up a reproach against their neighbors;
NRSVCE.4:
in whose eyes the wicked are despised,
but who honor those who fear the LORD;
who stand by their oath even to their hurt;
NRSVCE.5:
who do not lend money at interest,
and do not take a bribe against the innocent.
Those who do these things shall never be moved.

NET.1: LORD, who may be a guest in your home? Who may live on your holy hill?
NET.2: Whoever lives a blameless life, does what is right, and speaks honestly.
NET.3: He does not slander, or do harm to others, or insult his neighbor.
NET.4: He despises a reprobate, but honors the LORD’s loyal followers. He makes firm commitments and does not renege on his promise.
NET.5: He does not charge interest when he lends his money. He does not take bribes to testify against the innocent. The one who lives like this will never be upended.
